introduction: In West Bengal, registering a marriage ensures its legal validity under the law. The registration is facilitated via the centralized MARREG (Marriage Registration) Portal under the Law Department.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Typically 7-15 days for the Hindu Marriage Act. Special marriages require a 30-day notice period.
  • Application Fee: Standard physical registration fee sits around ₹100 - ₹200 depending on the Act applied.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Marriage Details, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Madhyamik Admit Card
  • Birth Certificate
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Voter ID
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Passport
Evidence of Marriage
  • Original wedding invitation card
  • Post-wedding photographs of the couple
Witnesses
  • Three witnesses
  • Witness ID proofs
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via MARREG Portal

West Bengal's MARREG portal (Registrar General of Marriages) provides an end-to-end online marriage registration service with GRIPS payment integration.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit MARREG Portal
    Go to rgmwb.gov.in/MARREG. This is the official West Bengal Marriage Registration portal.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "Register Your Marriage". Create a new account with your mobile number and email ID. Verify via OTP and log in.
  3. Fill Marriage Details
    Enter: groom's and bride's full names (as per Voter ID/Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, religion, residential address of both spouses, and complete details of 3 witnesses including their addresses and ID numbers.
  4. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies (JPG/PDF): age proof (Voter ID/Aadhaar/Birth Certificate), address proof, individual passport-size photos of bride and groom, joint wedding photo, wedding invitation, and 3 witness ID proofs.
  5. Pay via GRIPS Gateway
    Pay the registration fee via the integrated GRIPS (Government Receipt Portal System) payment gateway. Fee is approximately ₹100–₹500 depending on Act. Download the GRIPS receipt.
  6. Select Marriage Officer (MO) & Book Appointment
    Search for the Marriage Officer of your local jurisdiction. Submit the appointment request for physical verification at the MO's office.
  7. Attend MO's Office on Scheduled Date
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses must be physically present. Bring all originals. The MO verifies documents, records biometrics, and registers the marriage.
Download Certificate: After the MO completes registration, download the digitally signed Marriage Certificate from MARREG Portal using your application ID.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Local Marriage Officer's Office

Visit the local Marriage Officer (MO) or Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) of the area where the marriage was solemnized. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Identify the Correct MO / SRO
    The Marriage Officer for your area can be found through MARREG Portal → 'Find Marriage Officer'. Alternatively contact your District Registrar's office.
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain Form-A (under the West Bengal Special Marriages Act) or the relevant application from the MO's office. The form is free of cost.
  3. Fill All Details
    Enter: names of both spouses (as per Voter ID/Aadhaar), date and exact place of marriage, religion, district, and names, addresses, and ID details of 3 witnesses.
  4. Affix Photos & Notarized Affidavit
    Paste passport-size photographs on the form. Attach a notarized joint affidavit (on ₹50 stamp paper) confirming marital status and marriage details.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: age proof (Voter ID/Birth Certificate/Madhyamik Certificate), address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID), wedding invitation, wedding photos, and 3 witness ID proofs. Bring all originals.
  6. Submit & Pay Fee
    Submit the form at the MO's office. Pay the registration fee via Challan or GRIPS receipt. Collect the Acknowledgement Slip with your application number.
  7. Attend Verification & Collect Certificate
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses appear before the MO on the verification date. All sign the Marriage Register. Certificate is issued on the spot or available from MARREG Portal.
Helpline: West Bengal Registration Dept: 033-22483984. Processing time is typically 7–15 days after the verification date.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Physical Verification

Physical verification of both the couple and the three witnesses is still mandated at the registrar's office on the scheduled date.

Certificate Issue

Upon finalizing signatures on the physical register, an acknowledged digital copy/counter-signed physical copy is supplied to the couple.
